title: Home & Family
shortSummary: Talk about your family members and describe where you live.
culturalNote: Family meals, especially the long Sunday lunch, are a cherished French tradition where several generations gather. Family ties are warm but young adults often move out for study or work. In cities many people rent apartments, and the cosy, welcoming feel of a home is valued. Possessive adjectives and family vocabulary come up constantly in everyday chat.

keyPhrases.pronunciation: ah tew day FREHR ay SUHR

grammar.explanation: Use avoir to say what family or things you have: J'ai un frère (I have a brother). The article stays: un, une, des.
grammar.commonMistake: Using être for possession: it is J'ai un frère, not Je suis un frère.

grammar.explanation: Possessives agree with the gender of the family member: mon père (m), ma mère (f), mes parents (plural). Before a feminine noun starting with a vowel, use mon: mon amie.
grammar.commonMistake: Matching the possessive to yourself instead of the family member's gender.

dialogue.setup: Paul shows his friend Léa a family photo on his phone.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.native: Oui. Ce sont mes parents et ma sœur.
lines.english: Yes. These are my parents and my sister.

lines.speaker: Léa
lines.english: Do you have a brother too?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.native: Non, juste une sœur. Mais nous avons un chien.
lines.english: No, just a sister. But we have a dog.

lines.speaker: Léa
lines.native: Mignon ! Ta sœur habite encore chez vous ?
lines.english: Cute! Does your sister still live at home?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.native: Non, elle habite dans un appartement à Lyon.
lines.english: No, she lives in a flat in Lyon.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I have a brother'?
quiz.explanation: avoir is used for possession: J'ai un frère.

quiz.question: Which means 'my sister'?
quiz.explanation: sœur is feminine, so the possessive is ma.

quiz.question: As-tu des frères et _____ ? (sisters)
quiz.explanation: sœurs is the plural of sœur (sister).

quiz.question: Arrange: famille / c'est / ma
quiz.explanation: C'est (this is) plus the possessive phrase.
